description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with three stacked wings, painted in a light blue and white color scheme with dark blue cross insignias on the wings and tail. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers toward the rear, ending in a rounded vertical stabilizer and a single horizontal stabilizer. The front features a prominent propeller mounted on a circular engine cowling, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield for the pilot. The wings are supported by vertical struts and wires, giving the aircraft a classic, early 20th-century appearance. The overall design suggests a lightweight, maneuverable aircraft suitable for short flights or display.
description: A classic light blue and white triplane with open cockpit, three stacked wings, and vintage cross insignias, featuring a front-mounted propeller and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular engine cowling, propeller, and the three stacked wings with visible struts.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the open cockpit, cross insignia on the wings and tail, and the streamlined fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and the alignment of the three wings.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the cockpit, landing gear, and cross insignias.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller
attribute_tail_design: conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and a single horizontal stabilizer
attribute_landing_gear_type: taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a tail skid
attribute_cockpit_placement: forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing with a small windshield
